2606.26918.title: Diagnosing Task Insensitivity in Language Agents
2606.26918.paper_summary: Large language models can serve as capable long-horizon agents, but their out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization remains weak. We identify a key source of this failure as task insensitivity: when faced with similar but distinct tasks, models might apply patterns learned during training and fail to solve the task at hand. We show that models often continue with actions aligned with the original task even when the instruction is semantically corrupted and cannot be directly answered. We further find that, when we replace the task description in a trained prompt with another similar but distinct task, the model may still output the same action. This behavior is accompanied by a consistent training-time attention drift away from task tokens and toward local observations, suggesting an optimization bias toward shortcuts. To mitigate this problem, we propose Task-Perturbed NLL Optimization, a lightweight contrastive regularizer that explicitly encourages action dependence on the task instruction. Extensive evaluations show that our intervention improves task sensitivity and OOD generalization while preserving more stable attention to task tokens.
2606.26918.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型可以作為有能力的長期代理，但它們在分佈外（OOD）泛化方面仍然較弱。我們將這一失敗的主要原因確定為任務不敏感性：當面對相似但不同的任務時，模型可能會應用在訓練期間學到的模式，並無法解決當前的任務。我們顯示，模型經常在指令語義受損且無法直接回答的情況下，仍然繼續執行與原始任務一致的行動。我們進一步發現，當我們用另一個相似但不同的任務替換訓練提示中的任務描述時，模型仍然可能輸出相同的行動。這種行為伴隨著在訓練期間注意力持續偏離任務標記並朝向局部觀察，這表明存在對捷徑的優化偏見。為了減輕這個問題，我們提出了任務擾動NLL優化，這是一種輕量級的對比正則化器，明確鼓勵行動依賴於任務指令。廣泛的評估顯示，我們的干預改善了任務敏感性和OOD泛化，同時保持了對任務標記的更穩定注意力。
2606.26918.author: Jingyu Liu et.al.
2606.26918.authors: Jingyu Liu, Xiaopeng Wu, Kehan Chen, Chuan Yu, Yong Liu
